name: Elbmesh Reaction Workflow Graph
summary: Models cross-Resource behavior as Action -> Event -> Reaction -> Action graphs, with Reactions invoking Actions instead of mutating Resources directly.

rules: Represent cross-Resource behavior as Action -> Event -> Reaction -> Action.
Reaction conditions must be pure and subscribe only to successful Resource Events.
Reaction execution must call the same Action execution API used by direct callers.
Workflow is a named graph or subgraph over declared Reactions.
constraints: Do not let a Reaction mutate another Resource directly.
Do not introduce cyclic Reaction graphs.
Do not trigger Reactions from Action Journal, Reaction Journal, Operation Journal, or View records.
checks: Manifest Reactions reference known trigger Events and target Actions.
Manifest cycle checks still fail cyclic Action/Event/Reaction paths.
Reaction runtime uses deterministic reaction/action IDs for retries.
Reaction Journal records use StreamType::Reaction and do not enter Resource Event streams.
antiPatterns: Direct Resource mutation inside Reaction code.
Hidden Action-to-Action calls that skip Events.
Workflow state stored as a custom saga stream when it can be derived from Events and journals.

blocks.markdown: The accepted model is `Action -> Event -> Reaction -> Action`. Current code includes typed Reaction, ReactionRuntime, ReactionDispatcher, ReactionJournal, deterministic reaction action IDs, and manifest cycle checks. Restate-backed durable Reaction execution remains target architecture.

blocks.description: Accepted Reaction and Workflow graph model.
Current typed Reaction runtime and dispatcher.
Current Reaction Journal records and stream boundary.
Manifest validation for Reaction references and cycle detection.
Executable Reaction runtime and journal scenarios.
Offer-to-Invoice reference flow exercising declared Reactions and Views.
